summary: This brief post announces two new publications by Cathy O'Neil: a Bloomberg column titled 'When Algorithms Come for Our Children' and a book review for Science Magazine of Andreas Weigend's 'Data for the People.' The post serves primarily as a pointer to these external pieces rather than containing substantive analysis itself. The book review title suggests O'Neil found Weigend's perspective too narrowly focused on tech-insider viewpoints while overlooking equity concerns.
key_points: O'Neil has a new Bloomberg column focused on algorithms affecting children
She reviewed 'Data for the People' by Andreas Weigend for Science Magazine
Her review criticizes the book for neglecting issues of access and equality
The review title suggests tech insiders often have blind spots about who benefits from data systems
money_quotes: When Algorithms Come for Our Children
A tech insider's data dreams will resonate with the like-minded but neglect issues of access and equality
key_insight: Tech insider perspectives on data often fail to address fundamental questions of access and equality.
